MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-556678 WITH CASE CGC-15-547848
Off Calendar Jun-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS ROYAL FOOD STORE AND KIM HUYNH's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS CGC-17-556678 and CGC-15-547848 is OFF CALENDAR as moot in light of plaintiff Howard Green's expressed desire to dismiss case 556678 without prejudice. Mr. Green is required to file that dismissal no later than June 9, 2017. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene Tofft, CSR # 12313 =302/hk

Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jun-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS ROYAL FOOD STORE and KIM HUYNH'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
is DENIED as moot in light of the ruling granting plaintiff Howard Green's motion to set aside the dismissal in case 556678. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Irene Tofft, CSR # 12313 =302/hk

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DISMISSAL
Off Calendar Apr-10-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff Howard Green's motion to set aside the dismissal entered on January 25, 2017 is granted. Mr. Green's motion is also construed as seeking leave to file the second amended complaint that he filed on January 23, 2017 after the expiration of the time period he was given by the order sustaining the demurrer to his first amended complaint filed December 27, 2016 and, so construed, Mr. Green is granted leave and his second amended complaint is his operative pleading and defendants have 15 days from the filing of an order on this motion to respond to the second amended complaint. Under the circumstances of this case, Mr. Green's failure to file a second amended complaint within the time frame allowed by the December 27 order and his filing a request for dismissal without prejudice on January 25, 2017 constitute acts of excusable neglect per the discretionary relief provisions of CCP 473(b). Vacating the dismissal and granting leave to file the already filed second amended complaint comports with the strong policy of deciding cases on their merits rather than on technical defaults. Defendants Royal Food Store and Kim Moc Huynh have not shown that they will suffer legal prejudice by vacating the dismissal and granting leave to file the second amended complaint. Vacating the dismissal and granting leave to file the already filed second amended complaint returns this case to the posture it would have been in early January had Mr. Green not committed his two acts of excusable neglect. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR 711 =302/hk
